2606.07066.title: Modeling semantic association in self-paced reading with language model embeddings
2606.07066.paper_summary: Semantic association between a word and its context has been identified as an important component of reading comprehension, even when word predictability is accounted for. Recent research has highlighted the potential of language model ( LM) embeddings to quantify semantic association. Yet, embedding-based semantic association have been operationalized in a myriad of ways. In this study, we use embeddings from LMs to estimate semantic association on a corpus of joint electroencephalography (EEG) and self-paced reading of natural, Dutch texts. Semantic association is calculated in ten different implementations that vary the embedding model and context lengths. The effects of semantic association across the different implementations on the N400 and self-paced reading times are examined using Bayesian hierarchical models and Bayes factor. The results show that the choice of embedding model can alter the estimated effect of semantic association on both the N400 and self-paced reading times. Furthermore, the results demonstrate a promising potential of sentence embeddings for capturing semantic association, as only implementations relying on sentence embeddings indicate reliable results of semantic association beyond word predictability on both neural and behavioral measures. Together, these findings highlight the importance of methodological choices in quantifying semantic association.
2606.07066.paper_summary_zh: 語詞與其語境之間的語義關聯被認為是閱讀理解的重要組成部分，即使在考慮了詞彙可預測性的情況下也是如此。最近的研究突顯了語言模型（LM）嵌入量化語義關聯的潛力。然而，基於嵌入的語義關聯已經以多種方式進行操作。在這項研究中，我們使用來自語言模型的嵌入來估計一個包含聯合腦電圖（EEG）和自我調整閱讀自然荷蘭文本的語料庫中的語義關聯。語義關聯在十種不同的實現中計算，這些實現變化了嵌入模型和上下文長度。使用貝葉斯層次模型和貝葉斯因子檢驗不同實現中語義關聯對N400和自我調整閱讀時間的影響。結果顯示，嵌入模型的選擇可以改變對N400和自我調整閱讀時間的語義關聯估計效果。此外，結果顯示句子嵌入在捕捉語義關聯方面具有良好的潛力，因為只有依賴於句子嵌入的實現顯示出超越詞彙可預測性的語義關聯的可靠結果，無論是在神經測量還是行為測量上。綜合這些發現，強調了在量化語義關聯時方法選擇的重要性。
2606.07066.author: Sara Møller Østergaard et.al.
2606.07066.authors: Sara Møller Østergaard, Kenneth Enevoldsen, Afra Alishahi, Bruno Nicenboim
